name: Republic of Egypt
note: The Free Officers' Revolution (July 23, 1952) ended the monarchy. Nasser nationalised the Suez Canal (1956), triggering the Suez Crisis — and emerged as pan-Arab hero when US pressure forced Britain and France to withdraw. Nasser's Egypt was the champion of Arab nationalism. Sadat's Camp David Accords (1978) made peace with Israel — and got him assassinated (1981). Mubarak's 30-year autocracy ended with the Arab Spring (2011); democratic opening under Morsi crushed by military coup (2013) under Sisi.

figures.name: Nasser
figures.significance: The greatest Arab nationalist leader; nationalised the Suez Canal; champion of pan-Arabism; the dominant figure of 20th-century Arab politics

figures.name: Sadat
figures.significance: Expelled Soviet advisors (1972); surprised Israel in the Yom Kippur War (1973); made peace at Camp David (1978); Nobel Peace Prize; assassinated by Islamic Jihad at a military parade

figures.name: Sisi
figures.significance: Led the 2013 coup against Morsi; President since 2014; brutal suppression of Muslim Brotherhood; restored military's dominant role in Egyptian political economy
